text: The surgeon begins the dissection by using bipolar cautery to take down the inferior pulmonary ligament. A level nine lymph node is identified and carefully dissected out from the surrounding tissue.
The surgeon identifies and dissects out a level seven lymph node pocket. The anterior oblique fissure is divided using a pulper staple load, complemented by gentle blunt dissection.
The surgeon identifies and dissects out a level XI lymph node located within the oblique fissure. Above the node pocket, a clear passage is visible from the interlobar fissure to the posterior hilum.

The surgeon identifies the inferior lingual branch of the pulmonary artery within the interlobar fissure, circumferentially controls it using blunt dissection, and divides it. Subsequently, the superior lingual artery is also divided.

The bedside assistant introduces a wide load stapler through the posterior port. The surgeon completes the division of the posterior fissure using purple staple loads inserted through the anterior port.
The surgeon identifies the apico-posterior branch of the pulmonary artery, circumferentially controls it, and divides it within the surgical field.

The surgeon retracts the upper lobe posteriorly to isolate and divide the superior pulmonary vein, as the anatomical features prevent identification of the anterior branch of the pulmonary artery at this stage.
The surgeon divides the left upper lobe bronchus using electrocautery, providing clear exposure of the anterior branch of the pulmonary artery for safe identification and division.
The surgeon closes the left upper bronchial stem using a running IIIO absorbable V-lock suture, securing the bronchial stump with precise stitching.
The surgeon places the resected left upper lobe into a specimen retrieval bag and removes it through the previously made incision and the 15mm assistant port.

After the specimen removal, the surgeon performs a multilevel intercostal block and places a single 28 French chest tube through the camera port incision. The lung is inflated, and the remaining surgical wounds are closed.
